payload.query: The study of American Politics was dominated by the behavioral revolution during the 1960s and witnessed a reemergence of institutions in the 1990s. What is the direction of the study of American Politics over the next 5-10 years? What questions are ripe for answers in the 21st century?

articles.title: India and Pakistan Announce Cease-Fire but Clashes Persist
articles.abstract: President Trump also announced the truce, saying it had been mediated by the United States, although only Pakistan quickly acknowledged an American role.

articles.title: Pope’s Childhood in a Changing Chicago Tells a Story of Catholic America
articles.abstract: The pope grew up in a Catholic enclave on Chicago’s South Side. That community is gone now.

articles.title: What’s the Cost to Society of Pollution? Trump Says Zero.

articles.title: Plot Twists
articles.abstract: The best art makes us question the received ideas we’ve internalized and, just maybe, offers us ideas for living differently.

articles.title: Billionaires’ Battle Over a Sculpture Exposes a Mysterious Art Market
articles.abstract: David Geffen and Justin Sun’s unusually public dispute over ownership of a Giacometti sculpture valued at tens of millions of dollars gives a glimpse into a shrouded world.

articles.title: See Historical Records Documenting the Pope’s Creole Roots in New Orleans
articles.abstract: The documents, including census records, baptismal rolls and marriage certificates, trace the story of Pope Leo’s mother’s family and their diverse background before moving to Chicago.
articles.snippet: The documents, including census records, baptismal rolls and marriage certificates, trace the story of Pope Leo’s mother’s family and their diverse background before moving to Chicago.

articles.title: Margot Friedländer, Holocaust Survivor Who Found Her Voice, Dies at 103
articles.abstract: She never spoke of her experience until after her husband’s death, when she returned to Berlin with a mission to tell her story, and to teach tolerance.
articles.snippet: She never spoke of her experience until after her husband’s death, when she returned to Berlin with a mission to tell her story, and to teach tolerance.

articles.title: Voice of Lay Catholics Is Likely to Be Heard in Leo XIV’s Church
articles.abstract: When still a cardinal, the new pope led discussions on key issues facing the church during which “every voice had equal value,” whether an archbishop or an unordained believer.
